text: When passing towline to tug from mooring deck

options.text: Take full three turns on the bitts and throw the line towards tug
Pass both ends of the rope to the tug
Take half turn on bitts and pass one eye to the tug
Make fast both ends on board and pass the bight to the tug

explanation: When passing a towline to a tug, the ship's crew should take a half turn on the bitts and pass one eye of the rope to the tug. This allows for controlled tensioning and safe transfer of the towline.
